name: Bhai Prithipal & Mohanpal Singh (Patiala)
body: One of the most talented jathas of the 20th century was that of Bhai Prithipal Singh Ji and Bhai Mohanpal Singh Ji. They belong to the Rababi family of Bhai Ranjodh Singh Ji who served as Hazoori Raagi in Gurdwara Sahib Nankana Sahib before the division of Punjab. Bhai Ranjodh Singh Ji had three sons, the oldest being Bhai Pal Singh, followed by Bhai Jaswant Singh and the youngest, Bhai Gulzar Singh.  Bhai Prithipal Singh was the oldest son of Bhai Jaswant Singh, followed by Bhai Jasbir Singh, Bhai Mahinderpal Singh ‘Ladoo’ and Bhai Kishanpal Singh. Most are not aware that Bhai Mohanpal Singh was actually Bhai Gulzar Singh Ji’s son, making him Bhai Prithipal Ji’s cousin. Bhai Pal Singh Ji did not have any children of his own so both of his brothers gave the suffix of ‘Pal’ to their children as a sweet gesture to their elder brother. This tradition continues until today as most of the children in the family have the suffix of ‘Pal’ added to their names.

Being born into the Rababi tradition of music, both of them received their initial training from home but they also went on to learn the intricacies of classical music from doyens of the Patiala Gharana. Bhai Prithipal Singh Ji became a student of Ustad Abdul Rehman Khan who was the grandson of Ustad Ali Baksh Khan (of the famous duo of Allu Fatta) while Bhai Mohanpal Singh Ji went on to learn from one of the biggest names in classical music, Ustad Bade Ghulam Ali Khan.

They were also good friends with Ustad Salamat Ali Khan of Sham Chaurasi Gharana. Interestingly, the first time they met was during a ‘mehfil’ organised by a mutual acquaintance when Ustad Salamat Ali Khan visited Patiala. Both Bhai Mohanpal Ji and Bhai Prithipal Ji attended this function and after Khan Saab’s performance, they met with him. He had heard about them prior to this, so he requested them to sing and they obliged. I was told that Khan Saab sat and listened to them that evening for a good amount of time with full concentration. Since that day, they became very close and subsequently whenever Ustad Salamat Ali Khan Ji visited Patiala, he would stay with them.

I’d like to share another interesting story narrated to me by the late Bhai Harinder Singh Fakkar, who was their nephew. When my dad and I visited him in Bombay, he took us around the city and showed us the sights. One evening, we drove past the apartment of Lata Mangeshkar Ji and he told us that once when Bhai Mohanpal Ji came to Bombay, he had a wish to meet Lata Ji. He went to her apartment complex and was denied entry by the security guard on duty. So he did the next most logical thing, which was to stand in front of her balcony and sing in Raag Sindhi Bhairvi. When his voice reached Lata Ji’s ears, she came running to the balcony and invited him in. They spent some time sharing thoughts and experiences related to music and Lata Ji commented “Sardar Ji, you are the most ‘sureela’ Sardar I have ever heard”.

Despite receiving such recognition from some of the top artists, it is sad that our community did not recognise their talents and appreciate their efforts in maintaining such a high standard of Sangeet in the midst of its decline. Both of them lived and died in poverty. Bhai Prithipal Singh Ji passed away in 1993 followed by Bhai Mohanpal Singh Ji four years later. Both passed away at relatively young ages before attaining the age of 60.
Bhai Mohanpal Singh Ji also accompanied Bhai Shamsher Singh Zakhmi for a few tours in the 1980s. Those recordings will be added later on to a page dedicated to Bhai Shamsher Singh Zakhmi.
If anyone has more recordings of Bhai Prithipal Singh and Bhai Mohanpal Singh, or any of their family members, please contact us.

The following recordings of Bhai Mohanpal Singh Ji were recorded in someone’s home. These were contributed by Sahib Singh Bakshi who has his own Youtube channel. Please visit his channel for more treasures of the past. https://www.youtube.com/user/sahibsingh100
